title: Unicode, Language, Tokenization, Fields, and Citation Offsets · Program Brief: Rag Unicode Normalization Policy
summary: A search service lowercases and compatibility-normalizes every field, tokenizes all languages on spaces, removes legal negation as a stop word, and can no longer link a hit to its source span. Predict the stakeholder decision, corpus and query identity, relevance assumption, failure cost, and evidence that would falsify the proposed RAG claim before opening the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the unicode normalization policy increment in reversible multilingual text pipeline.…
objectives: Choose and version Unicode normalization per field while retaining original text and testing canonical and compatibility distinctions.
Quarantine malformed, unauthorized, poisoned, executable, hidden, or policy-violating content and always treat accepted content as untrusted data.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: rag-unicode-normalization-policy — Choose and version Unicode normalization per field while retaining original text and testing canonical and compatibility distinctions.
# Evidence: rag-worked-rag-normalization-tokenization-4
# Competency: rag-worked-rag-normalization-tokenization-4.
# Case 1655: an incident-response knowledge search.
# Operating constraint: measure retrieval and generation separately before accepting an end-to-end score.
# Changed case: double corpus size and candidate depth while preserving the latency, memory, and cost envelope.
def worked_rag_normalization_tokenization_4(original: str = "Café API v2", normalized: str = "café api v2", language: str = "fr", field_count: int = 3, offsets_preserved: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "corpusworked4",
        "query4dekrow",
        "tenant1655",
        "indexworked41655",
        "prompt4dekrow1655",
        "evaluation1655worked4",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if not original or not normalized:
        return False, 0, "empty-text"
    if not language or field_count < 1:
        return False, field_count, "language-fields"
    if not offsets_preserved:
        return False, len(normalized), "citation-offsets"
    tokens = tuple(part for part in normalized.split() if part)
    if not tokens:
        return False, 0, "tokenization"
    assert "v2" in tokens
    return True, len(tokens), "unicode-field-model"

content.code:
# Evidence: rag-unicode-normalization-policy-pkxxnr
# Competency: rag-unicode-normalization-policy.
# Case 1612: a public-benefits policy change.
# Operating constraint: record the failed stage, causal trace, repair, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: change tenant, language, date, and document version and reject stale or unauthorized evidence.
def evidence_rag_unicode_normalization_policy_pkxxnr(original: str = "Café API v2", normalized: str = "café api v2", language: str = "fr", field_count: int = 3, offsets_preserved: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_pkxxnr = "pkxxnr-rnxxkp-1612"
    assert evidence_variant_pkxxnr
    evidence_axes_pkxxnr = (
        "corpuspkxxnr",
        "queryrnxxkp",
        "tenant1612",
        "indexpkxxnr1612",
        "promptrnxxkp1612",
        "evaluation1612pkxxnr",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_pkxxnr)) == 6
    if not original or not normalized:
        return False, 0, "empty-text"
    if not language or field_count < 1:
        return False, field_count, "language-fields"
    if not offsets_preserved:
        return False, len(normalized), "citation-offsets"
    tokens = tuple(part for part in normalized.split() if part)
    if not tokens:
        return False, 0, "tokenization"
    assert "v2" in tokens
    return True, len(tokens), "unicode-field-model"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Choose and version Unicode normalization per field while retaining original text and testing canonical and compatibility distinctions.

hints: For RAG and Python Defend the Rag Unicode Normalization Policy model · Theory Rag Unicode Normalization…, name the stakeholder, corpus, source, document, chunk, model, index, query, relevance, tenant, context, citation, evaluation, and transfer boundary, Rag Unicode Normalization Policy precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At RAG and Python Defend the Rag Unicode Normalization Policy model · Theory Rag Unicode Normalization…, trace this concrete input and predict ingestion, normalization, retrieval, filter, fusion, reranking, context, claim, citation, abstention, latency, cost, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory RAG engineering team handles case 6137 during an incident-response knowledge search. Build deterministic Python evidence to normalize and tokenize multilingual fields without losing source text or citation offsets; retain corpus, document, chunk, model, index, prompt, query, and evaluation version identity; then replace one exact identifier with a synonym and compare lexical, dense, and hybrid recall. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Choose and version Unicode normalization per field while retaining original text and testing canonical and compatibility distinctions.

For RAG and Python Defend the Rag Unicode Normalization Policy model · Theory Rag Unicode Normalization…, challenge the documented misconception with changed-query, changed-corpus, relevance, retrieval, grounding, citation, adversarial, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native or production-transfer evidence: NFKC is harmless for every natural-language and identifier field.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: rag-unicode-normalization-policy-pkxxnr.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: rag-unicode-normalization-policy-pkxxnr(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_rag_unicode_normalization_policy_pkxxnr\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not original)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?field_count < 1)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not offsets_preserved)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?normalized\.split)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
